# {{project_name}} Repository Blueprint

## Purpose

- Product outcome this repository owns: {{outcome}}
- Primary users/operators: {{actors}}
- Explicit non-goals: {{non_goals}}

## Executable entry points

| Entry point | Responsibility | Inputs/outputs | Owner |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| {{path_or_command}} | {{single responsibility}} | {{contract}} | {{team_or_role}} |

## Module boundaries

| Module/package | Owns | Public interface | May depend on | Must not depend on |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| {{module}} | {{behavior_or_data}} | {{exports_or_contract}} | {{allowed}} | {{forbidden}} |

Prefer organization by product capability. Keep domain behavior independent from HTTP, UI, persistence, and vendor SDKs where that boundary makes behavior easier to test or change.

## Required repository surfaces

- `README.md`: fast orientation and links, not the full source of truth.
- `docs/product.md`: users, problem, outcome, scope, non-goals, success.
- `docs/architecture.md`: current boundaries, contracts, data, operations, security.
- `docs/decisions/`: consequential decision history.
- Agent instructions: setup, verification, ownership, and safety constraints.
- Repeatable commands for install, build, typecheck, test, lint/format, and release checks.

## Developer workflow

1. Prerequisites: {{runtime_and_services}}
2. Install: `{{install_command}}`
3. Configure: {{configuration_without_secrets}}
4. Run: `{{run_command}}`
5. Verify: `{{verification_commands}}`
6. Build artifact: `{{build_command}}`

## Quality gates

| Gate | Command/evidence | Blocks merge? | Owner |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Types/contracts | {{command}} | {{yes_no}} | {{owner}} |
| Unit/integration | {{command}} | {{yes_no}} | {{owner}} |
| Security/dependencies | {{command_or_review}} | {{yes_no}} | {{owner}} |
| Production build | {{command}} | {{yes_no}} | {{owner}} |

## Operations and recovery

- Environments and deployment artifact: {{details}}
- Configuration and secret ownership: {{details}}
- Health, logs, metrics, traces, and alerts: {{details}}
- Migration ordering: {{details}}
- Rollback or forward-recovery procedure: {{details}}
- Incident owner and escalation: {{details}}

## Repository completion check

- [ ] A new contributor can run and verify the project from committed instructions.
- [ ] Ownership and dependency direction are visible from the tree and public interfaces.
- [ ] Generated, secret, build, and local state are excluded intentionally.
- [ ] CI executes the same meaningful commands developers run locally.
- [ ] Production-impacting changes have rollout, observability, and recovery evidence.
